doc.prompt: Write a 300+ word summary of the wikipedia page "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Raymond_III,_Count_of_Tripoli". Do not use any commas and highlight at least 3 sections that has titles in markdown format, for example *highlighted section part 1*, *highlighted section part 2*, *highlighted section part 3*.

arg_0: [{"role": "user", "content": "Write a 300+ word summary of the wikipedia page \"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Raymond_III,_Count_of_Tripoli\". Do not use any commas and highlight at least 3 sections that has titles in markdown format, for example *highlighted section part 1*, *highlighted section part 2*, *highlighted section part 3*.", "type": "text"}]

resps:
*Early Life and Background*
Raymond III was a prominent nobleman who served as the Count of Tripoli during the twelfth century. He was the son of Pons of Tripoli and he inherited his title through his family lineage. His life was defined by the complex political landscape of the Crusader States where power struggles between local lords and foreign leaders were constant. He was known for his military leadership and his ability to navigate the treacherous waters of feudal politics. His reign was marked by both conflict and diplomacy as he sought to maintain the stability of his county against various threats from surrounding territories.

*Conflict and Political Struggles*
The political life of Raymond III was often characterized by intense rivalry with other leaders such as King Guy of Lusignan. These disputes frequently centered on the control of Jerusalem and the rightful succession of leadership within the Latin East. He often found himself at odds with the crown which led to periods of civil unrest and shifting alliances. Despite these tensions he remained a central figure in the defense of the Crusader territories. His strategic decisions influenced the survival of the County of Tripoli for many years. He worked to balance the needs of his vassals with the demands of the higher authorities while ensuring his own domain remained secure.

*Legacy and Death*
Raymond III left a significant mark on the history of the Levant through his long tenure and his involvement in major historical events. His death marked the end of an era for the County of Tripoli as his successors had to deal with the increasing pressure from surrounding Muslim powers. Historians view him as a complex figure who embodied the struggles of the Crusader nobility. He was a leader who fought to preserve his family legacy amidst constant warfare. His influence can still be seen when studying the intricate power dynamics of the medieval Middle East. He remains a key subject for those interested in the history of the Crusades and the political evolution of the Levant.

doc.prompt: I am planning a trip to Japan, and I would like thee to write an itinerary for my journey in a Shakespearean style. You are not allowed to use any commas in your response.

arg_0: [{"role": "user", "content": "I am planning a trip to Japan, and I would like thee to write an itinerary for my journey in a Shakespearean style. You are not allowed to use any commas in your response.", "type": "text"}]

resps:
Hark traveler of the rising sun!

Thy quest begins in Tokyo where neon lights do blaze like fallen stars upon the earth. Thou shalt wander through Shibuya where the crowds do surge like a mighty sea. Then seek the shrines of Meiji where silence reigns supreme amidst the trees.

Next thou shalt ride the iron steed of Shinkansen toward the mountain of Fuji. Its snowy peak doth pierce the heavens with a majesty most divine. Thou shalt dwell in Kyoto where ancient spirits dwell in golden halls. Walk through the bamboo groves of Arashai where the wind doth whisper secrets of old.

Then travel south to Osaka where the feasts are grand and the streets are bright. Eat thy fill of takoyaki while the moon doth watch thy joy.

Finally thou shalt find peace in the temples of Nara where deer do roam like gentle ghosts. Thy journey ends with a heavy heart but a soul filled with light.

Go forth now with courage and may thy path be ever blessed!
